summary: Iter7 — Weekly Recap feature tested end-to-end. Backend: 5/5 pytest passed (recap/this-week generates valid JSON with all required keys + 2145-char essay; second call returns cached recap in <2s; /recap/history sorted desc; /recap/regenerate produces new id same week_str; all existing endpoints — auth/me, profile, dashboard/stats, dashboard/pain-points, drill/streak, badges, speaking/topics, writing/prompts — still 200). Frontend at /app/recap: page renders with title 'Week of Jun 15–21 — Pattern Lock', large serif headline, 3-paragraph essay (2145 chars in recap-essay), 4 common errors (recap-errors with pattern/example/fix), 5 top vocab cards (recap-vocab with word/definition/example/best_used_in), next-week-focus chip, and recap-wallpaper at 300x540 dark gradient. Dashboard recap-teaser button is positioned AFTER daily-drill-card and navigates to /app/recap when clicked. recap-pdf-btn invokes window.print(). recap-wallpaper-btn renders via html2canvas and triggers data:image/png download (verified Blob/data URL created). recap-regen-btn becomes disabled during regeneration (loading state). No JS console errors. The pre-existing Recharts width/height warnings on dashboard are unchanged from iter6 (cosmetic).

issues: Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) console warnings persist (cosmetic, carried over from iter6). Charts still render.

critical_code_review_comments: recap_service.py — uses week_label as session_id for _build_chat: recap-{week_label} contains spaces/em-dash; if the LLM client sanitises keys this is fine but using week_str (e.g. 2026-W25) would be more robust.
server.py _week_label uses '%-d' which is Linux-only (not portable to Windows) — acceptable for this Docker/Linux deploy.

server.py /api/recap/regenerate calls recap_this_week(user_id=user_id) directly bypassing the Depends() — this works because the function uses user_id as a default-arg name; but it's slightly fragile if signature changes. Consider extracting the cache-or-build logic into a helper called by both endpoints.
Recap.jsx — printableRef is declared but never used (only wallpaperRef is passed to downloadPNG). Dead code, can be removed.
Recap.jsx — html2canvas import (~50KB) is loaded eagerly on /app/recap even if user never exports; consider dynamic import() for code-splitting.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all recap UI + integration flows verified

seed_data_creation: Generated 2 weekly recaps for current ISO week (one from initial call, then replaced by /recap/regenerate). History now contains 1 recap for this week.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Owner has a fresh weekly recap cached for this ISO week (Jun 15-21, 2026 in test env). /api/recap/this-week returns instantly (<2s) due to cache. To force regeneration, hit POST /api/recap/regenerate (~25-30s). Empty-state path (400 'Not enough activity...') was NOT tested per spec instructions to avoid wiping DB. The recap-empty data-testid block exists in Recap.jsx if needed.
